---
title: Projet ECRIN
subtitle: Analyse de la première vague d’enquêtes
date: today
lang: fr
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: Sommaire
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
table {
  margin-left: auto;
  margin-right: auto;
}
table.conf-aud {
  table-layout: fixed;
  width: auto;
}
table.conf-aud th:nth-child(1),
table.conf-aud td:nth-child(1) { width: 12em; text-align: left; }
table.conf-aud th:nth-child(2),
table.conf-aud td:nth-child(2) {
  width: 3em; text-align: center;
  border-left: 1px solid #dee2e6;
  border-right: 1px solid #dee2e6;
}
table.conf-aud th:nth-child(n+3),
table.conf-aud td:nth-child(n+3) { width: 8em; text-align: center; }
</style>
```

```{r}
#| label: setup
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

project_dir <- dirname(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE))

data_raw <- read_delim(
  file.path(project_dir, "..", "ECRINV1_DATA_2026-03-18_1018.csv"),
  delim = ";", show_col_types = FALSE, name_repair = "minimal"
)

data_labels <- read_delim(
  file.path(project_dir, "..", "ECRINV1_DATA_LABELS_2026-03-18_1113.csv"),
  delim = ";", show_col_types = FALSE, name_repair = "minimal"
)
```

Le projet ECRIN vise à faciliter les interactions entre chercheurs. Dans ce but, une enquête a été mise en place à l’aide d’un questionnaire en ligne. Ce questionnaire est administré avec le logiciel [REDCap](https://www.project-redcap.org).

Notre enquête comporte quatre formulaires, ou instruments selon la terminologie de REDCap, chaque instrument correspondant à une thématique. Les participants sont invités par courriel à répondre de manière successive et dans le même ordre à ces instruments. 

## Participation

```{r}
#| label: n-participants
n_participants <- nrow(data_raw)
```


Sur 45 personnes contactées, `r n_participants` chercheurs ont souhaité participer à notre première vague d’enquêtes.

```{r}
#| label: participation-instruments
instruments <- c(
  "researcher_profile_complete",
  "research_questions_complete",
  "publications_complete",
  "project_proposal_complete"
)
labels <- c(
  "Profil chercheur",
  "Questions de recherche",
  "Publications",
  "Proposition de projet"
)

tibble(
  Instrument = labels,
  Complété = map_int(instruments, ~ sum(data_raw[[.x]] == 2, na.rm = TRUE)),
  `Non complété` = map_int(instruments, ~ sum(data_raw[[.x]] != 2 | is.na(data_raw[[.x]]), na.rm = TRUE))
) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

### Conclusion

La participation est très élevée — 100% des volontaires ont répondu — sauf pour l’instrument lié à la proposition de projet. Ce dernier instrument est différent des autres et demande beaucoup d’investissement à l’enquêté.

Nous sommes en présence d’une communauté active, ce qui est de bonne augure pour la suite du projet.

## Profil du chercheur

```{r}
#| label: portrait-choix-camembert
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
choix_portrait <- c(
  "1" = "Avatar généré",
  "2" = "Photo de moi",
  "3" = "Rien"
)

pie_portrait <- data_raw |>
  filter(researcher_profile_complete == 2) |>
  mutate(choix = choix_portrait[as.character(portrait_choice)]) |>
  count(choix) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n), label = paste0(n, "\n(", round(pct * 100), "%)")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = choix)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = guide_legend(nrow = 2)) +
  labs(title = "Choix du portrait", fill = NULL) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

prof_inst <- read_csv(
  file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads", "researcher_profile", "identifiables.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

abrev_inst <- c(
  "Université Le Havre Normandie (ULHN)" = "ULHN",
  "Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique (CNRS)" = "CNRS",
  "Universidade dos Açores (UAC)" = "UAC"
)

pie_institution <- prof_inst |>
  mutate(
    inst_full = if_else(!is.na(institution_other) & institution_other != "", institution_other, institution),
    inst = if_else(inst_full %in% names(abrev_inst), abrev_inst[inst_full], inst_full)
  ) |>
  count(inst) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n), label = paste0(n, "\n(", round(pct * 100), "%)")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = inst)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  labs(title = "Affiliations", fill = NULL) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(pie_portrait, pie_institution, ncol = 2)
```

### Trombinoscope

```{r}
#| label: trombinoscope
#| results: asis
identifiables <- read_csv(
  file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads", "researcher_profile", "identifiables.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

img_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads", "researcher_profile", "files")

all_profiles <- identifiables |>
  arrange(last_name, first_name) |>
  mutate(
    middle_part = if_else(!is.na(middle_name) & middle_name != "", paste0(" ", middle_name), ""),
    full_name   = paste0(str_to_title(first_name), middle_part, " ", str_to_title(last_name)),
    img_src = case_when(
      portrait_choice == "A picture of me" ~ {
        p <- file.path(img_dir, paste0(hashed_id, "_portrait.", tools::file_ext(portrait)))
        if_else(file.exists(p), p, paste0("https://api.dicebear.com/9.x/shapes/svg?seed=", hashed_id))
      },
      portrait_choice == "An avatar (automatically generated)" ~
        paste0("https://api.dicebear.com/9.x/shapes/svg?seed=", hashed_id),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cards <- all_profiles |>
  mutate(
    card = if_else(
      is.na(img_src),
      paste0(
        '<div style="text-align:center;display:inline-block;width:130px;margin:8px;vertical-align:top;">',
        '<div style="width:110px;height:110px;"></div>',
        '<div style="font-size:0.8em;margin-top:4px;">', full_name, '</div>',
        '</div>'
      ),
      paste0(
        '<div style="text-align:center;display:inline-block;width:130px;margin:8px;vertical-align:top;">',
        '<img src="', img_src, '" style="width:110px;height:110px;object-fit:cover;border-radius:50%;"/>',
        '<div style="font-size:0.8em;margin-top:4px;">', full_name, '</div>',
        '</div>'
      )
    )
  )

htmltools::browsable(htmltools::HTML(paste0(
  '<div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;justify-content:center;">',
  paste(cards$card, collapse = ""),
  '</div>'
)))
```

### ORCID

```{r}
#| label: orcid-table
identifiables |>
  arrange(last_name, first_name) |>
  mutate(
    middle_part = if_else(!is.na(middle_name) & middle_name != "", paste0(" ", middle_name), ""),
    Nom = paste0(str_to_title(first_name), middle_part, " ", str_to_title(last_name)),
    ORCID = if_else(
      !is.na(orcid) & str_trim(orcid) != "",
      paste0(
        '<a href="https://orcid.org/', str_trim(orcid), '" target="_blank">',
        str_trim(orcid), '</a>'
      ),
      "—"
    )
  ) |>
  mutate(`#` = row_number()) |>
  select(`#`, Nom, ORCID) |>
  knitr::kable(format = "html", escape = FALSE) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## Questions de recherche

Les questions de recherche ont été analysées par modélisation thématique (LDA, 5 topics) et pondération TF-IDF.

```{r}
#| label: nlp-setup
#| include: false
nlp_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads",
                     "research_questions", "nlp-research_questions")

lda_topics  <- read_csv(file.path(nlp_dir, "lda_topics.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
tfidf       <- read_csv(file.path(nlp_dir, "tfidf.csv"),      show_col_types = FALSE)

enrichi_path <- file.path(nlp_dir, "lda_individus_enrichi.csv")
if (file.exists(enrichi_path)) {
  lda_indiv <- read_csv(enrichi_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    mutate(
      full_name      = paste0(str_to_title(first_name), " ", str_to_title(last_name)),
      topic_dominant = as.character(topic_dominant)
    )
} else {
  lda_indiv <- read_csv(file.path(nlp_dir, "lda_individus.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    mutate(
      full_name      = as.character(userid),
      topic_dominant = as.character(topic_dominant)
    )
}

topic_colors <- c("1" = "#e41a1c", "2" = "#377eb8", "3" = "#4daf4a",
                  "4" = "#ff7f00", "5" = "#984ea3")
has_lda <- nrow(lda_topics) > 0
```

### Top termes par topic

```{r}
#| label: nlp-top-termes
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7
if (has_lda) {
  lda_topics |>
    filter(langue == "en") |>
    mutate(topic = paste("Topic", topic)) |>
    group_by(topic) |>
    slice_max(beta, n = 10) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(term = paste0(term, "__", topic),
           term = fct_reorder(term, beta)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = term, y = beta, fill = topic)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.85) +
    facet_wrap(~ topic, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) sub("__.*$", "", x)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(unname(topic_colors), paste("Topic", 1:5))) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Probabilité (β)", title = "Top 10 termes par topic (LDA)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
}
```

### Nuages de mots par topic

```{r}
#| label: nlp-wordcloud
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8
#| warning: false
if (has_lda) {
  par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(1, 1, 2, 1))
  for (t in 1:5) {
    df <- lda_topics |>
      filter(langue == "en", topic == t) |>
      select(word = term, freq = beta) |>
      arrange(desc(freq))
    wordcloud::wordcloud(
      words = df$word, freq = df$freq,
      max.words = 10, scale = c(3, 0.8),
      colors = colorRampPalette(c("grey80", topic_colors[[as.character(t)]]))(10),
      main = paste("Topic", t)
    )
    title(paste("Topic", t), line = 0.5)
  }
}
```

### Heatmap topics × chercheurs

```{r}
#| label: nlp-heatmap
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7
if (has_lda) {
  lda_indiv |>
    select(full_name, topic_dominant, gamma_1:gamma_5) |>
    pivot_longer(gamma_1:gamma_5, names_to = "topic", values_to = "gamma") |>
    mutate(
      topic     = paste("Topic", str_extract(topic, "\\d")),
      full_name = factor(full_name, levels = lda_indiv |>
                           arrange(topic_dominant, full_name) |> pull(full_name))
    ) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = topic, y = full_name, fill = gamma)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_text(aes(label = if_else(gamma > 0.15, round(gamma, 2), NA_real_)),
              size = 3, color = "white") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7f7f7", high = "#2166ac", name = "γ") +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = "Appartenance thématique par chercheur (γ)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
          panel.grid  = element_blank())
}
```

### TF-IDF — termes discriminants

```{r}
#| label: nlp-tfidf
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 7
if (nrow(tfidf) > 0) {
  tfidf |>
    filter(langue == "en") |>
    slice_max(tf_idf_moyen, n = 30) |>
    mutate(token = fct_reorder(token, tf_idf_moyen)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = token, y = tf_idf_moyen, size = n_docs, color = n_docs)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "#c6dbef", high = "#08519c", name = "Nb docs") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10), name = "Nb docs") +
    guides(size = "none") +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "TF-IDF moyen", title = "Top 30 termes discriminants (TF-IDF)") +
    theme_minimal()
}
```

### Réseau chercheurs — topics

```{r}
#| label: nlp-network
if (has_lda) {
  library(visNetwork)

  nodes_topics <- tibble(
    id    = paste0("t", 1:5),
    label = paste("Topic", 1:5),
    group = "topic",
    color = unname(topic_colors),
    shape = "ellipse",
    size  = 30,
    font.size = 16
  )

  nodes_chercheurs <- lda_indiv |>
    mutate(id = paste0("c", row_number())) |>
    select(id, label = full_name, topic_dominant) |>
    mutate(
      group = "chercheur",
      color = topic_colors[topic_dominant],
      shape = "dot",
      size  = 18,
      font.size = 13
    )

  nodes <- bind_rows(nodes_topics, nodes_chercheurs |> select(-topic_dominant))

  gamma_long <- lda_indiv |>
    mutate(from = paste0("c", row_number())) |>
    select(from, gamma_1:gamma_5) |>
    pivot_longer(-from, names_to = "topic", values_to = "gamma") |>
    mutate(to = paste0("t", str_extract(topic, "\\d"))) |>
    filter(gamma > 0.05) |>
    mutate(value = round(gamma * 10, 1), title = paste0("γ = ", round(gamma, 2)))

  edges <- gamma_long |> select(from, to, value, title)

  visNetwork(nodes, edges, height = "550px", width = "100%") |>
    visPhysics(
      solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
      forceAtlas2Based = list(gravitationalConstant = -80, springLength = 120)
    ) |>
    visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE)) |>
    visLegend(addNodes = list(
      list(label = "Topic",     shape = "ellipse", color = "#aaa"),
      list(label = "Chercheur", shape = "dot",     color = "#aaa")
    ), useGroups = FALSE) |>
    visInteraction(navigationButtons = TRUE)
}
```

## Publications

Les noms de co-auteurs sont extraits automatiquement par NLP depuis les listes de publications fournies au format PDF. La qualité de l'extraction dépend de la mise en forme de chaque document : les formats hétérogènes (abréviation des prénoms, ordre nom/prénom variable, caractères spéciaux, artefacts de PDF) peuvent introduire des doublons ou des noms mal parsés. Le graphe ci-dessous est donc indicatif et non exhaustif.

### Analyse des titres extraits

```{r}
#| label: publi-nlp-setup
#| include: false
pub_nlp_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads",
                         "publications", "nlp-publications")

pub_source  <- read_csv(file.path(pub_nlp_dir, "00_source.csv"),    show_col_types = FALSE)
pub_topics  <- read_csv(file.path(pub_nlp_dir, "lda_topics.csv"),   show_col_types = FALSE)
pub_indivs  <- read_csv(file.path(pub_nlp_dir, "lda_individus.csv"),show_col_types = FALSE)
pub_tfidf   <- read_csv(file.path(pub_nlp_dir, "tfidf.csv"),        show_col_types = FALSE)
bib_refs    <- read_csv(file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads",
                                  "publications", "extracted-publications", "biblio_refs.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

pub_topic_colors <- c("1" = "#e41a1c", "2" = "#377eb8", "3" = "#4daf4a",
                      "4" = "#ff7f00", "5" = "#984ea3")

prof_ids <- read_csv(file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads",
                               "researcher_profile", "identifiables.csv"),
                     show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  select(hashed_id, last_name, first_name) |>
  mutate(full_name = paste0(str_to_title(first_name), " ", str_to_title(last_name)))
```

#### Distribution des langues

```{r}
#| label: publi-langues
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4
lang_labels <- c(
  en = "Anglais", fr = "Français", unknown = "Indéterminée",
  ja = "Japonais", ca = "Catalan", cy = "Gallois",
  de = "Allemand", la = "Latin", bs = "Bosnien",
  mt = "Maltais", ny = "Chichewa", fy = "Frison"
)

pub_source |>
  mutate(langue_label = coalesce(lang_labels[langue], langue)) |>
  count(langue_label) |>
  mutate(
    langue_label = fct_reorder(langue_label, n),
    pct = n / sum(n),
    label = paste0(n, " (", round(pct * 100), "%)")
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = langue_label, y = n, fill = langue_label)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 145)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Anglais" = "#2166ac", "Français" = "#d6604d",
    "Indéterminée" = "#aaaaaa"
  ), na.value = "#cccccc") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Nombre de titres",
       title = "Distribution des langues détectées",
       subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(pub_source), " titres extraits")) +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### Distribution de la longueur des titres

```{r}
#| label: publi-longueur
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 4
pub_source |>
  mutate(langue_grp = case_when(
    langue == "en"      ~ "Anglais",
    langue == "fr"      ~ "Français",
    langue == "unknown" ~ "Indéterminée",
    TRUE                ~ "Autre"
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = n_tokens, fill = langue_grp)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, color = "white", alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Anglais" = "#2166ac", "Français" = "#d6604d",
    "Indéterminée" = "#aaaaaa", "Autre" = "#74c476"
  )) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 3, linetype = "dashed", color = "#888", linewidth = 0.6) +
  annotate("text", x = 3.5, y = Inf, label = "Seuil artefact", vjust = 1.5,
           hjust = 0, size = 3, color = "#888") +
  labs(x = "Nombre de mots", y = "Nombre de titres", fill = "Langue",
       title = "Longueur des titres extraits",
       subtitle = "Les titres très courts (≤ 3 mots) sont probablement des artefacts d'extraction") +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### Top termes par topic

```{r}
#| label: publi-top-termes
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 8
pub_topics |>
  filter(langue %in% c("en", "fr")) |>
  mutate(
    langue_label = if_else(langue == "en", "Anglais", "Français"),
    topic_label  = paste("Topic", topic)
  ) |>
  group_by(langue_label, topic_label) |>
  slice_max(beta, n = 8) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(term = paste0(term, "__", langue_label, "_", topic_label),
         term = fct_reorder(term, beta)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = term, y = beta, fill = topic_label)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(langue_label ~ topic_label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) sub("__.*$", "", x)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(unname(pub_topic_colors), paste("Topic", 1:5))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "β", title = "Top 8 termes par topic — Titres de publications (LDA)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

#### TF-IDF — termes discriminants

```{r}
#| label: publi-tfidf
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6
pub_tfidf |>
  filter(langue %in% c("en", "fr")) |>
  mutate(langue_label = if_else(langue == "en", "Anglais", "Français")) |>
  group_by(langue_label) |>
  slice_max(tf_idf_moyen, n = 20) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(token = fct_reorder(token, tf_idf_moyen)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = token, y = tf_idf_moyen, size = n_docs, color = n_docs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~ langue_label, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#c6dbef", high = "#08519c", name = "Nb docs") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "TF-IDF moyen",
       title = "Top 20 termes discriminants des titres (TF-IDF)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### Heatmap chercheurs × topics

```{r}
#| label: publi-heatmap
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6
pub_indivs |>
  left_join(bib_refs |> select(ref_id, hashed_id), by = c("userid" = "ref_id")) |>
  filter(!is.na(hashed_id)) |>
  left_join(prof_ids, by = "hashed_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(full_name)) |>
  mutate(topic_dominant = as.character(topic_dominant)) |>
  select(full_name, topic_dominant, gamma_1:gamma_5) |>
  group_by(full_name) |>
  summarise(
    topic_dominant = names(which.max(table(topic_dominant))),
    across(gamma_1:gamma_5, mean),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(gamma_1:gamma_5, names_to = "topic", values_to = "gamma") |>
  mutate(
    topic     = paste("Topic", str_extract(topic, "\\d")),
    full_name = factor(full_name, levels = {
      pub_indivs |>
        left_join(bib_refs |> select(ref_id, hashed_id), by = c("userid" = "ref_id")) |>
        left_join(prof_ids, by = "hashed_id") |>
        filter(!is.na(full_name)) |>
        mutate(topic_dominant = as.character(topic_dominant)) |>
        group_by(full_name) |>
        summarise(td = names(which.max(table(topic_dominant))), .groups = "drop") |>
        arrange(td) |>
        pull(full_name)
    })
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = topic, y = full_name, fill = gamma)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = if_else(gamma > 0.15, round(gamma, 2), NA_real_)),
            size = 3, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7f7f7", high = "#8e0152", name = "γ moyen") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
       title = "Appartenance thématique des chercheurs — Publications (γ moyen)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"), panel.grid = element_blank())
```

#### Réseau thématique publications

```{r}
#| label: publi-network
library(visNetwork)
pub_indivs_ecrin <- pub_indivs |>
  left_join(bib_refs |> select(ref_id, hashed_id), by = c("userid" = "ref_id")) |>
  filter(!is.na(hashed_id)) |>
  left_join(prof_ids, by = "hashed_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(full_name)) |>
  mutate(topic_dominant = as.character(topic_dominant))

nodes_topics_pub <- tibble(
  id    = paste0("t", 1:5),
  label = paste("Topic", 1:5),
  group = "topic",
  color = unname(pub_topic_colors),
  shape = "ellipse",
  size  = 30,
  font.size = 16
)

nodes_cherch_pub <- pub_indivs_ecrin |>
  select(full_name, topic_dominant) |>
  distinct(full_name, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    id        = full_name,
    label     = full_name,
    group     = "chercheur",
    color     = pub_topic_colors[topic_dominant],
    shape     = "dot",
    size      = 18,
    font.size = 13
  )

nodes_pub <- bind_rows(
  nodes_topics_pub,
  nodes_cherch_pub |> select(id, label, group, color, shape, size, font.size)
)

gamma_long_pub <- pub_indivs_ecrin |>
  select(full_name, gamma_1:gamma_5) |>
  group_by(full_name) |>
  summarise(across(gamma_1:gamma_5, mean), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_longer(-full_name, names_to = "topic", values_to = "gamma") |>
  mutate(to = paste0("t", str_extract(topic, "\\d"))) |>
  filter(gamma > 0.05) |>
  transmute(from = full_name, to, value = round(gamma * 10, 1),
            title = paste0("γ = ", round(gamma, 2)))

visNetwork(nodes_pub, gamma_long_pub, height = "550px", width = "100%") |>
  visPhysics(
    solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
    forceAtlas2Based = list(gravitationalConstant = -80, springLength = 120)
  ) |>
  visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE)) |>
  visLegend(addNodes = list(
    list(label = "Topic",     shape = "ellipse", color = "#aaa"),
    list(label = "Chercheur", shape = "dot",     color = "#aaa")
  ), useGroups = FALSE) |>
  visInteraction(navigationButtons = TRUE)
```

### Réseau de co-auteurs

```{r}
#| label: coauteurs-network
library(visNetwork)

pub_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads",
                     "publications", "extracted-publications")

bib_authors <- read_csv(file.path(pub_dir, "biblio_authors.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Pour chaque référence : hashed_id ECRIN + liste co-auteurs
refs_authors <- bib_authors |>
  group_by(ref_id, hashed_id) |>
  summarise(authors = list(author), .groups = "drop")

# Arêtes ECRIN → co-auteur, pondérées par nb d'articles communs
edges_raw <- refs_authors |>
  unnest(authors) |>
  rename(coauthor = authors) |>
  left_join(prof_ids |> select(hashed_id, ecrin_name = full_name), by = "hashed_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(ecrin_name)) |>
  # exclure l'auteur ECRIN lui-même si son nom apparaît dans la liste
  filter(coauthor != ecrin_name) |>
  count(ecrin_name, coauthor, name = "weight")

# Nœuds ECRIN
ecrin_names <- unique(edges_raw$ecrin_name)
nodes_ecrin <- tibble(
  id    = ecrin_names,
  label = ecrin_names,
  group = "ECRIN",
  color = "#2166ac",
  shape = "dot",
  size  = 22,
  font.size = 13,
  font.bold = TRUE
)

# Nœuds co-auteurs (garder ceux avec >= 2 co-publications pour lisibilité)
coauthor_weight <- edges_raw |>
  group_by(coauthor) |>
  summarise(total = sum(weight)) |>
  filter(total >= 2)

nodes_coauth <- coauthor_weight |>
  transmute(
    id    = coauthor,
    label = coauthor,
    group = "Co-auteur",
    color = "#d6604d",
    shape = "dot",
    size  = pmin(6 + total * 2, 20),
    font.size = 11,
    font.bold = FALSE
  )

nodes <- bind_rows(nodes_ecrin, nodes_coauth)

edges <- edges_raw |>
  filter(coauthor %in% coauthor_weight$coauthor) |>
  transmute(
    from  = ecrin_name,
    to    = coauthor,
    value = weight,
    title = paste0(weight, " article", if_else(weight > 1, "s", ""))
  )

visNetwork(nodes, edges, height = "600px", width = "100%") |>
  visPhysics(
    solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
    forceAtlas2Based = list(gravitationalConstant = -60, springLength = 140, springConstant = 0.05)
  ) |>
  visOptions(
    highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE),
    nodesIdSelection = list(enabled = TRUE, main = "Sélectionner un chercheur")
  ) |>
  visLegend(addNodes = list(
    list(label = "Chercheur ECRIN", shape = "dot", color = "#2166ac", size = 15),
    list(label = "Co-auteur (≥ 2 articles)", shape = "dot", color = "#d6604d", size = 10)
  ), useGroups = FALSE) |>
  visInteraction(navigationButtons = TRUE)
```

## Proposition de projet

### Topics, mots-clés, méthodes et régions

```{r}
#| label: proj-setup
#| include: false
proj_full <- read_csv(
  file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads", "project_proposal", "identifiables.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

rq_enrichi_path <- file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads",
                             "research_questions", "nlp-research_questions", "lda_individus_enrichi.csv")
rq_lda <- if (file.exists(rq_enrichi_path)) {
  read_csv(rq_enrichi_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    mutate(full_name = paste0(str_to_title(first_name), " ", str_to_title(last_name)),
           topic_lda = paste("Topic LDA", topic_dominant))
} else {
  read_csv(
    file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads",
              "research_questions", "nlp-research_questions", "lda_individus.csv"),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  ) |>
    mutate(full_name = as.character(userid),
           topic_lda = paste("Topic LDA", topic_dominant))
}

# Table longue : un chercheur × une valeur par type
proj_long <- proj_full |>
  left_join(prof_ids, by = "hashed_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(full_name)) |>
  left_join(rq_lda |> select(full_name, topic_lda), by = "full_name") |>
  select(full_name, topic_lda, topic,
         keyword1, keyword2, keyword3,
         method1, method2, method3,
         region1, region2, region3) |>
  pivot_longer(keyword1:region3, names_to = "var", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value) & value != "") |>
  mutate(type = case_when(
    startsWith(var, "keyword") ~ "Mot-clé",
    startsWith(var, "method")  ~ "Méthode",
    startsWith(var, "region")  ~ "Région"
  ))
```

#### Heatmap topics × mots-clés

```{r}
#| label: proj-heatmap-kw
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 6
proj_long |>
  filter(type == "Mot-clé", !is.na(topic_lda)) |>
  count(topic_lda, value) |>
  group_by(topic_lda) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 8) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(value = fct_reorder(value, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = topic_lda, y = value, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 3.5, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#c6dbef", high = "#08519c", name = "N") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = "Mots-clés les plus fréquents par topic LDA") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid = element_blank())
```

#### Diagramme de Sankey — topic → mot-clé → méthode → région

```{r}
#| label: proj-sankey
library(networkD3)

make_sankey_links <- function(data, from_col, to_col, min_n = 1) {
  data |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[from_col]]) & !is.na(.data[[to_col]])) |>
    count(.data[[from_col]], .data[[to_col]]) |>
    filter(n >= min_n) |>
    rename(source = 1, target = 2, value = n)
}

proj_sankey <- proj_full |>
  left_join(prof_ids, by = "hashed_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(full_name)) |>
  left_join(rq_lda |> select(full_name, topic_lda), by = "full_name")

links_tk  <- make_sankey_links(proj_sankey, "topic_lda", "keyword1")
links_km  <- make_sankey_links(proj_sankey |>
  pivot_longer(keyword1:keyword3, values_to = "kw", names_to = "kvar") |>
  pivot_longer(method1:method3,   values_to = "meth", names_to = "mvar") |>
  filter(!is.na(kw) & kw != "" & !is.na(meth) & meth != ""), "kw", "meth")
links_mr  <- make_sankey_links(proj_sankey |>
  pivot_longer(method1:method3, values_to = "meth", names_to = "mvar") |>
  pivot_longer(region1:region3, values_to = "reg",  names_to = "rvar") |>
  filter(!is.na(meth) & meth != "" & !is.na(reg) & reg != ""), "meth", "reg", min_n = 1)

all_links <- bind_rows(links_tk, links_km, links_mr)
all_nodes <- tibble(name = unique(c(all_links$source, all_links$target)))

all_links <- all_links |>
  mutate(
    source = match(source, all_nodes$name) - 1L,
    target = match(target, all_nodes$name) - 1L
  )

sankeyNetwork(
  Links = as.data.frame(all_links),
  Nodes = as.data.frame(all_nodes),
  Source = "source", Target = "target", Value = "value",
  NodeID = "name", fontSize = 11, nodeWidth = 20,
  height = 600, width = "100%",
  sinksRight = FALSE
)
```

#### Bubble chart — topics × mots-clés

```{r}
#| label: proj-bubble
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7
proj_long |>
  filter(type == "Mot-clé", !is.na(topic)) |>
  count(topic, value) |>
  group_by(topic) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 5) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = topic, y = value, size = n, color = topic)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(4, 14), name = "N chercheurs") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", guide = "none") +
  labs(x = "Thématique (topic déclaré)", y = "Mot-clé",
       title = "Mots-clés par thématique déclarée") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))
```

#### Réseau de co-occurrence — mots-clés, méthodes, régions

```{r}
#| label: proj-cooccurrence
cooc_data <- proj_full |>
  left_join(prof_ids, by = "hashed_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(full_name)) |>
  left_join(rq_lda |> select(full_name, topic_lda), by = "full_name") |>
  select(full_name, topic_lda, keyword1:keyword3, method1:method3, region1:region3) |>
  pivot_longer(-c(full_name, topic_lda), names_to = "var", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value) & value != "") |>
  mutate(type = case_when(
    startsWith(var, "keyword") ~ "kw",
    startsWith(var, "method")  ~ "meth",
    startsWith(var, "region")  ~ "reg"
  ))

cooc_edges <- cooc_data |>
  inner_join(cooc_data, by = "full_name", relationship = "many-to-many") |>
  filter(value.x < value.y) |>
  count(value.x, value.y, type.x, type.y, name = "weight") |>
  filter(weight >= 2)

type_colors <- c("kw" = "#0d6efd", "meth" = "#6f42c1", "reg" = "#0dcaf0")

all_vals <- unique(c(cooc_edges$value.x, cooc_edges$value.y))
node_types <- cooc_data |>
  distinct(value, type) |>
  filter(value %in% all_vals)

nodes_cooc <- tibble(id = all_vals) |>
  left_join(node_types, by = c("id" = "value")) |>
  mutate(
    label     = id,
    color     = type_colors[type],
    shape     = "dot",
    size      = 12,
    font.size = 11,
    group     = type
  )

edges_cooc <- cooc_edges |>
  transmute(from = value.x, to = value.y, value = weight,
            title = paste0(weight, " chercheur(s)"))

visNetwork(nodes_cooc, edges_cooc, height = "600px", width = "100%") |>
  visPhysics(solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
             forceAtlas2Based = list(gravitationalConstant = -50, springLength = 100)) |>
  visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE)) |>
  visLegend(addNodes = list(
    list(label = "Mot-clé",  shape = "dot", color = "#0d6efd", size = 12),
    list(label = "Méthode",  shape = "dot", color = "#6f42c1", size = 12),
    list(label = "Région",   shape = "dot", color = "#0dcaf0", size = 12)
  ), useGroups = FALSE) |>
  visInteraction(navigationButtons = TRUE)
```

#### Réseau tripartite — chercheurs, topics, mots-clés/méthodes/régions

```{r}
#| label: proj-tripartite
topic_lda_colors <- setNames(
  c("#e41a1c","#377eb8","#4daf4a","#ff7f00","#984ea3"),
  paste("Topic LDA", 1:5)
)

nodes_cherch_tri <- rq_lda |>
  filter(full_name %in% proj_full$hashed_id |
           full_name %in% (proj_full |> left_join(prof_ids, by="hashed_id") |> pull(full_name))) |>
  semi_join(proj_full |> left_join(prof_ids, by="hashed_id") |> filter(!is.na(full_name)),
            by = "full_name") |>
  distinct(full_name, topic_lda) |>
  transmute(id = full_name, label = full_name, group = "chercheur",
            color = topic_lda_colors[topic_lda], shape = "dot", size = 18, font.size = 12)

nodes_topics_tri <- tibble(
  id    = paste("Topic LDA", 1:5),
  label = paste("Topic LDA", 1:5),
  group = "topic",
  color = unname(topic_lda_colors),
  shape = "ellipse", size = 28, font.size = 14
)

val_nodes <- cooc_data |>
  distinct(value, type) |>
  transmute(id = value, label = value, group = type,
            color = type_colors[type], shape = "dot", size = 10, font.size = 10)

nodes_tri <- bind_rows(nodes_cherch_tri, nodes_topics_tri, val_nodes)

edges_ct <- rq_lda |>
  semi_join(nodes_cherch_tri, by = c("full_name" = "id")) |>
  transmute(from = full_name, to = topic_lda, value = 3, title = topic_lda)

edges_cv <- cooc_data |>
  semi_join(nodes_cherch_tri, by = c("full_name" = "id")) |>
  transmute(from = full_name, to = value, value = 1, title = type)

edges_tri <- bind_rows(edges_ct, edges_cv)

visNetwork(nodes_tri, edges_tri, height = "650px", width = "100%") |>
  visPhysics(solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
             forceAtlas2Based = list(gravitationalConstant = -70, springLength = 150)) |>
  visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE),
             nodesIdSelection = list(enabled = TRUE, main = "Sélectionner")) |>
  visLegend(addNodes = list(
    list(label = "Chercheur", shape = "dot",     color = "#aaa",    size = 14),
    list(label = "Topic LDA", shape = "ellipse", color = "#aaa",    size = 14),
    list(label = "Mot-clé",   shape = "dot",     color = "#0d6efd", size = 10),
    list(label = "Méthode",   shape = "dot",     color = "#6f42c1", size = 10),
    list(label = "Région",    shape = "dot",     color = "#0dcaf0", size = 10)
  ), useGroups = FALSE) |>
  visInteraction(navigationButtons = TRUE)
```

#### Réseau sigmajs — chercheur / topic / keyword / méthode / région

```{r}
#| label: proj-sigma
#| out-width: "100%"
library(sigmajs)

sigma_data <- proj_full |>
  left_join(prof_ids, by = "hashed_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(full_name)) |>
  select(full_name, topic, keyword1:keyword3, method1:method3, region1:region3)

type_pal <- c(
  chercheur = "#2166ac",
  topic     = "#d6604d",
  keyword   = "#4dac26",
  method    = "#7b2d8b",
  region    = "#e08214"
)

nodes_sigma <- bind_rows(
  sigma_data |> distinct(full_name) |>
    transmute(id = full_name, label = full_name, node_type = "chercheur"),
  sigma_data |> filter(!is.na(topic) & topic != "") |> distinct(topic) |>
    transmute(id = topic, label = topic, node_type = "topic"),
  sigma_data |> pivot_longer(keyword1:keyword3, values_to = "v") |>
    filter(!is.na(v) & v != "") |> distinct(v) |>
    transmute(id = v, label = v, node_type = "keyword"),
  sigma_data |> pivot_longer(method1:method3, values_to = "v") |>
    filter(!is.na(v) & v != "") |> distinct(v) |>
    transmute(id = v, label = v, node_type = "method"),
  sigma_data |> pivot_longer(region1:region3, values_to = "v") |>
    filter(!is.na(v) & v != "") |> distinct(v) |>
    transmute(id = v, label = v, node_type = "region")
) |>
  distinct(id, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    color = type_pal[node_type],
    size  = case_when(
      node_type == "chercheur" ~ 5,
      node_type == "topic"     ~ 4,
      TRUE                     ~ 2
    ),
    x = runif(n(), -1, 1),
    y = runif(n(), -1, 1)
  )

edges_sigma <- bind_rows(
  sigma_data |> filter(!is.na(topic) & topic != "") |>
    transmute(id = paste0("ct_", row_number()), source = full_name, target = topic),
  sigma_data |> pivot_longer(keyword1:keyword3, values_to = "v") |>
    filter(!is.na(v) & v != "") |>
    transmute(id = paste0("ck_", row_number()), source = topic, target = v),
  sigma_data |> pivot_longer(method1:method3, values_to = "v") |>
    filter(!is.na(v) & v != "") |>
    transmute(id = paste0("cm_", row_number()), source = topic, target = v),
  sigma_data |> pivot_longer(region1:region3, values_to = "v") |>
    filter(!is.na(v) & v != "") |>
    transmute(id = paste0("cr_", row_number()), source = topic, target = v)
) |>
  mutate(
    source = as.character(source),
    target = as.character(target)
  ) |>
  filter(source %in% nodes_sigma$id & target %in% nodes_sigma$id)

w <- sigmajs(height = "600px", width = "100%") |>
    sg_nodes(nodes_sigma, id, label, size, color, x, y) |>
    sg_edges(edges_sigma, id, source, target) |>
    sg_layout(layout = igraph::layout_with_fr) |>
    sg_settings(
      defaultEdgeColor    = "#cccccc",
      edgeColor           = "default",
      minNodeSize         = 2,
      maxNodeSize         = 8,
      labelThreshold      = 1,
      drawEdges           = TRUE,
      defaultLabelColor   = "#333333"
    ) |>
    sg_neighbors()

# sigmajs 0.1.5 ignore le paramètre height — on force via CSS sur l'id du widget
wid <- w$elementId %||% paste0("sigmajs-", sample.int(1e6, 1))
w$elementId <- wid
htmltools::tagList(
  htmltools::tags$style(sprintf(
    "#%s { height: 600px !important; width: 100%% !important; }
     #%s canvas { height: 600px !important; width: 100%% !important; }",
    wid, wid
  )),
  w,
  htmltools::tags$script(sprintf(
    "document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
       setTimeout(function() {
         var el = document.getElementById('%s');
         if (el) {
           el.style.height = '600px';
           el.style.width  = '100%%';
           var canvases = el.querySelectorAll('canvas');
           canvases.forEach(function(c) {
             c.style.height = '600px';
             c.height = 600;
             c.style.width  = '100%%';
           });
           if (window.HTMLWidgets) {
             HTMLWidgets.staticRender();
           }
         }
       }, 500);
     });",
    wid
  ))
)
```

### Intégration des projets dans ceux de l’Université

```{r}
#| label: ecr-eunicoast
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
labels_ecr <- c(
  "ecr_integration___1" = "Enjeux maritimes et portuaires",
  "ecr_integration___2" = "La ville de demain : architecture, urbanisme,\nconstruction durable et design",
  "ecr_integration___3" = "Transitions, risques et aléas"
)

labels_eunicoast <- c(
  "eunicoast_integration___1" = "Identités, savoirs locaux et\npatrimoine culturel",
  "eunicoast_integration___2" = "Économie circulaire bleue, logistique\nportuaire et tourisme bleu durable",
  "eunicoast_integration___3" = "Gouvernance, planification,\ngestion et surveillance",
  "eunicoast_integration___4" = "Santé, biodiversité et solutions\nfondées sur la nature",
  "eunicoast_integration___5" = "Solutions ingéniées et numériques\npour les infrastructures côtières"
)

completed <- data_raw |> filter(researcher_profile_complete == 2)
n_proj <- nrow(completed)

plot_integration <- function(data, labels, titre, fill_color) {
  cols <- names(labels)
  data |>
    select(all_of(cols)) |>
    summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(as.integer(.x) == 1, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n") |>
    mutate(
      label = labels[variable],
      label = factor(label, levels = rev(labels)),
      pct   = n / n_proj
    ) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = label, y = n)) +
    geom_col(fill = fill_color, alpha = 0.85, width = 0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", round(pct * 100), "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, n_proj + 2), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks()) +
    labs(title = titre, x = NULL, y = paste0("Nombre de projets (N=", n_proj, ")")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
}

p_ecr      <- plot_integration(completed, labels_ecr,      "Intégration ECR",      "#fd7e14")
p_eunicoast <- plot_integration(completed, labels_eunicoast, "Intégration EUNICoast", "#e83e8c")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_ecr, p_eunicoast, ncol = 2)
```

## Fiches chercheurs

```{r}
#| label: portfolio-setup
#| include: false
base_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "..", ".ecrin", "admin", "downloads")

prof   <- read_csv(file.path(base_dir, "researcher_profile",  "identifiables.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
rq     <- read_csv(file.path(base_dir, "research_questions",  "identifiables.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
publi  <- read_csv(file.path(base_dir, "publications",        "identifiables.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
proj   <- read_csv(file.path(base_dir, "project_proposal",    "identifiables.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

img_dir_portrait <- file.path(base_dir, "researcher_profile", "files")
img_dir_publi    <- file.path(base_dir, "publications",        "files")
img_dir_proj     <- file.path(base_dir, "project_proposal",   "files")

portrait_file <- function(hid, portrait_name) {
  ext  <- tools::file_ext(portrait_name)
  path <- file.path(img_dir_portrait, paste0(hid, "_portrait.", ext))
  if (file.exists(path)) path else NA_character_
}

render_portrait <- function(path, hid, choice, size = "90px") {
  if (choice == "An avatar (automatically generated)") {
    src <- paste0("https://api.dicebear.com/9.x/shapes/svg?seed=", hid)
    glue::glue('<img src="{src}" style="width:{size};height:{size};object-fit:cover;border-radius:50%;border:2px solid #dee2e6;"/>')
  } else if (!is.na(path)) {
    glue::glue('<img src="{path}" style="width:{size};height:{size};object-fit:cover;border-radius:50%;border:2px solid #dee2e6;"/>')
  } else {
    glue::glue('<div style="width:{size};height:{size};"></div>')
  }
}

render_tags_outline <- function(items, color = "#0d6efd") {
  items <- items[!is.na(items) & items != ""]
  if (length(items) == 0) return("")
  paste0(
    '<span style="display:inline-block;background:transparent;color:', color,
    ';border:1px solid ', color, ';border-radius:4px;padding:1px 7px;margin:2px;font-size:0.8em;">',
    items, '</span>',
    collapse = ""
  )
}

render_tags <- function(items, color = "#0d6efd") {
  items <- items[!is.na(items) & items != ""]
  if (length(items) == 0) return("")
  paste0(
    '<span style="display:inline-block;background:', color, '22;color:', color,
    ';border:1px solid ', color, '44;border-radius:4px;padding:1px 7px;margin:2px;font-size:0.8em;">',
    items, '</span>',
    collapse = ""
  )
}

render_publi_link <- function(hid) {
  files <- list.files(img_dir_publi, pattern = paste0("^", hid, "_publications\\."), full.names = TRUE)
  if (length(files) == 0) return("")
  path <- files[[1]]
  ext  <- toupper(tools::file_ext(path))
  glue::glue('<a href="{path}" style="font-size:0.85em;">\U0001f4c4 Publications</a>')
}

render_proposal_papers <- function(hid, papers) {
  papers <- papers[!is.na(papers) & papers != ""]
  if (length(papers) == 0) return("")
  links <- purrr::imap_chr(papers, function(label, i) {
    pattern <- paste0("^", hid, "_paper", i, "\\.")
    files   <- list.files(img_dir_proj, pattern = pattern, full.names = TRUE)
    if (length(files) > 0) {
      path <- files[[1]]
      glue::glue('<a href="{path}" style="font-size:0.85em;">\U0001f4c4 Article {i}</a>')
    } else {
      glue::glue('<span style="font-size:0.85em;color:#aaa;">\U0001f4c4 Article {i}</span>')
    }
  })
  paste(links, collapse = " | ")
}

section_html <- function(title, content, color = "#495057") {
  if (is.null(content) || is.na(content) || content == "") return("")
  glue::glue(
    '<div style="margin-top:10px;">',
    '<div style="font-weight:600;color:{color};font-size:0.85em;text-transform:uppercase;',
    'letter-spacing:.05em;margin-bottom:4px;">{title}</div>',
    '<div>{content}</div>',
    '</div>'
  )
}
```

```{r}
#| label: portfolio-render
#| results: asis
cards_html <- purrr::map_chr(prof |> arrange(last_name, first_name) |> pull(userid), function(uid) {
  p   <- prof |> filter(userid == uid)
  hid <- p$hashed_id

  # Portrait
  img_path     <- if (!is.na(p$portrait) && p$portrait != "") portrait_file(hid, p$portrait) else NA_character_
  portrait_html <- render_portrait(img_path, hid, coalesce(p$portrait_choice, ""))

  # Identit\u00e9
  middle    <- if (!is.na(p$middle_name) && p$middle_name != "") paste0(" ", p$middle_name) else ""
  full_name <- paste0(str_to_title(p$first_name), middle, " ", str_to_title(p$last_name))
  institution <- if (!is.na(p$institution_other) && p$institution_other != "") p$institution_other else p$institution

  orcid_val  <- str_trim(coalesce(p$orcid, ""))
  orcid_html <- if (orcid_val != "") {
    orcid_url <- if (startsWith(orcid_val, "http")) orcid_val else paste0("https://orcid.org/", orcid_val)
    glue::glue('<a href="{orcid_url}" style="font-size:0.8em;color:#a6ce39;">\u2b21 ORCID</a>')
  } else ""

  # Questions de recherche
  rq_row  <- rq |> filter(userid == uid)
  rq_html <- if (nrow(rq_row) > 0 && !is.na(rq_row$research_questions) && rq_row$research_questions != "") {
    paste0('<div style="font-size:0.85em;font-style:italic;">', rq_row$research_questions, '</div>')
  } else ""

  # Intégrations ECR / EUNICoast
  raw_row <- data_raw |> filter(userid == uid)
  ecr_choix <- c(
    "ecr_integration___1" = "Enjeux maritimes et portuaires",
    "ecr_integration___2" = "La ville de demain : architecture, urbanisme, construction durable et design",
    "ecr_integration___3" = "Transitions, risques et aléas"
  )
  eunicoast_choix <- c(
    "eunicoast_integration___1" = "Identités, savoirs locaux et patrimoine culturel",
    "eunicoast_integration___2" = "Économie circulaire bleue, logistique portuaire et tourisme bleu durable",
    "eunicoast_integration___3" = "Gouvernance, planification, gestion et surveillance",
    "eunicoast_integration___4" = "Santé, biodiversité et solutions fondées sur la nature",
    "eunicoast_integration___5" = "Solutions ingéniées et numériques pour les infrastructures côtières"
  )
  ecr_vals       <- if (nrow(raw_row) > 0) unname(ecr_choix[names(ecr_choix)[sapply(names(ecr_choix),      function(v) isTRUE(as.integer(raw_row[[v]][[1]]) == 1))]]) else character(0)
  eunicoast_vals <- if (nrow(raw_row) > 0) unname(eunicoast_choix[names(eunicoast_choix)[sapply(names(eunicoast_choix), function(v) isTRUE(as.integer(raw_row[[v]][[1]]) == 1))]]) else character(0)
  ecr_html       <- section_html("Intégration ECR",       render_tags(ecr_vals,       "#fd7e14"), "#fd7e14")
  eunicoast_html <- section_html("Intégration EUNICoast", render_tags(eunicoast_vals, "#e83e8c"), "#e83e8c")

  # Publications
  refs_html <- render_publi_link(hid)

  # Proposition de projet
  proj_row  <- proj |> filter(userid == uid)
  proj_html <- ""
  if (nrow(proj_row) > 0) {
    kw     <- c(proj_row$keyword1, proj_row$keyword2, proj_row$keyword3)
    meth   <- c(proj_row$method1,  proj_row$method2,  proj_row$method3)
    reg    <- c(proj_row$region1,  proj_row$region2,  proj_row$region3)
    papers <- c(proj_row$paper1,   proj_row$paper2,   proj_row$paper3)

    title_proj <- ""
    if (!is.na(proj_row$title) && proj_row$title != "") {
      acronym_part <- if (!is.na(proj_row$acronym) && proj_row$acronym != "") {
        paste0('<span style="display:inline-block;background:#198754;color:#fff;border-radius:4px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:0.85em;font-weight:700;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;">', proj_row$acronym, '</span>')
      } else ""
      title_proj <- paste0(acronym_part, '<span style="font-size:1em;font-weight:600;font-style:italic;color:#198754;">', proj_row$title, '</span>')
    }

    abstract_proj <- if (!is.na(proj_row$abstract) && proj_row$abstract != "") {
      paste0('<div style="font-size:0.83em;margin-top:6px;color:#555;">', proj_row$abstract, '</div>')
    } else ""

    papers_html <- render_proposal_papers(hid, papers)

    topic_html <- if (!is.na(proj_row$topic) && proj_row$topic != "") {
      render_tags(proj_row$topic, "#20c997")
    } else ""

    logo_dir <- file.path(project_dir)

    integration_html <- ""
    parts <- c()
    if (length(ecr_vals) > 0) {
      parts <- c(parts, paste0(
        '<div style="display:flex;align-items:flex-start;gap:8px;margin-bottom:4px;">',
        '<img src="cptmp.png" style="height:24px;object-fit:contain;margin-top:3px;flex-shrink:0;"/>',
        '<div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:flex-start;">',
        render_tags_outline(ecr_vals, "#8B0000"),
        '</div></div>'
      ))
    }
    if (length(eunicoast_vals) > 0) {
      parts <- c(parts, paste0(
        '<div style="display:flex;align-items:flex-start;gap:8px;margin-bottom:4px;">',
        '<img src="eunicoast.png" style="height:24px;object-fit:contain;margin-top:3px;flex-shrink:0;"/>',
        '<div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:flex-start;">',
        render_tags_outline(eunicoast_vals, "#8B0000"),
        '</div></div>'
      ))
    }
    if (length(parts) > 0) {
      integration_html <- section_html(
        "Int\u00e9gration dans les projets de l\u2019Universit\u00e9",
        paste(parts, collapse = ""),
        "#8B0000"
      )
    }

    proj_inner <- paste0(
      title_proj, abstract_proj,
      integration_html,
      section_html("Th\u00e9matique", topic_html, "#20c997"),
      section_html("Mots-cl\u00e9s",          render_tags(kw,   "#0d6efd"), "#0d6efd"),
      section_html("M\u00e9thodes",            render_tags(meth, "#6f42c1"), "#6f42c1"),
      section_html("R\u00e9gions",             render_tags(reg,  "#0dcaf0"), "#0dcaf0"),
      if (papers_html != "") section_html("Articles soumis", papers_html, "#795548") else ""
    )
    proj_html <- section_html("Proposition de projet", proj_inner, "#000000")

  }

  glue::glue(
    '<div style="border:1px solid #dee2e6;border-radius:8px;padding:20px;margin-bottom:24px;',
    'background:#fff;box-shadow:0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,.06);">',
    '  <div style="display:flex;align-items:flex-start;gap:16px;">',
    '    <div style="flex-shrink:0;">{portrait_html}</div>',
    '    <div style="flex:1;min-width:0;">',
    '      <div style="font-size:1.15em;font-weight:700;">{full_name}</div>',
    '      <div style="color:#6c757d;font-size:0.9em;">{institution}</div>',
    '      <div style="margin-top:2px;">{orcid_html}</div>',
    '    </div>',
    '  </div>',
    '  {section_html("Questions de recherche", rq_html, "#000000")}',
    '  {section_html("Publications", refs_html, "#000000")}',
    '  {proj_html}',
    '</div>'
  )
})

htmltools::browsable(htmltools::HTML(paste(cards_html, collapse = "\n")))
```

## Niveau de confidentialité et audience

Pour chaque instrument, nous proposons à l’enquêté de choisir un niveau de confidentialité parmi trois options :

- données identifiables (nom visible), 
- pseudonymisées (un identifiant remplace le nom), 
- ou agrégées uniquement (statistiques globales sans données individuelles). 

Il choisit également l’audience autorisée à accéder à ses données :

- les chercheurs authentifiés uniquement,
- ou le grand public.

```{r}
#| label: confidentialite-tableau
instruments_conf <- list(
  "Profil chercheur"        = c("researcher_profile_identification_level", "researcher_profile_data_audience"),
  "Questions de recherche"  = c("research_questions_identification_level", "research_questions_data_audience"),
  "Publications"            = c("publications_identification_level", "publications_data_audience"),
  "Proposition de projet"   = c("project_proposal_identification_level", "project_proposal_data_audience")
)

niveaux_conf <- c(
  "1" = "Identifiable",
  "2" = "Pseudonymisé",
  "3" = "Anonymisé",
  "4" = "Agrégé uniquement"
)
ordre_conf <- c("Identifiable", "Pseudonymisé", "Anonymisé", "Agrégé uniquement")

niveaux_aud <- c(
  "1" = "Grand public",
  "2" = "Chercheurs authentifiés"
)

ordre_aud <- c("Grand public", "Chercheurs authentifiés")

complete_cols <- c(
  "Profil chercheur"       = "researcher_profile_complete",
  "Questions de recherche" = "research_questions_complete",
  "Publications"           = "publications_complete",
  "Proposition de projet"  = "project_proposal_complete"
)

pct <- function(counts, niveaux) {
  vals <- paste0(round(as.integer(counts) / sum(as.integer(counts)) * 100), "%")
  setNames(as.list(vals), niveaux)
}

map_dfr(names(instruments_conf), function(inst) {
  conf <- factor(niveaux_conf[as.character(data_raw[[instruments_conf[[inst]][1]]])], levels = ordre_conf)
  n    <- sum(data_raw[[complete_cols[inst]]] == 2, na.rm = TRUE)
  as_tibble(pct(table(conf)[ordre_conf], ordre_conf)) |>
    mutate(Instrument = inst, `N` = n, .before = 1)
}) |>
  kable(caption = "Confidentialité", align = c("l", "c", rep("c", length(ordre_conf)))) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, htmltable_class = "conf-aud")

map_dfr(names(instruments_conf), function(inst) {
  aud <- factor(niveaux_aud[as.character(data_raw[[instruments_conf[[inst]][2]]])], levels = ordre_aud)
  n   <- sum(data_raw[[complete_cols[inst]]] == 2, na.rm = TRUE)
  as_tibble(pct(table(aud)[ordre_aud], ordre_aud)) |>
    mutate(Instrument = inst, `N` = n, .before = 1)
}) |>
  kable(caption = "Audience", align = c("l", "c", rep("c", length(ordre_aud)))) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, htmltable_class = "conf-aud")
```

### Comportements observés

Les participants sont regroupés par classification ascendante hiérarchique (CAH) sur la base de leurs choix de confidentialité et d'audience pour les quatre instruments. La dissimilarité entre individus est mesurée par la distance de Gower, adaptée aux variables ordinales à échelles mixtes. L'agrégation suit la méthode UPGMA (*average linkage*), qui fusionne les groupes selon la dissimilarité moyenne entre leurs membres — un choix cohérent avec une matrice de Gower non euclidienne, contrairement à la méthode de Ward. Le dendrogramme est découpé en 5 groupes.

```{r}
#| label: comportements-hclust
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
cols_behav <- c(
  "researcher_profile_identification_level", "researcher_profile_data_audience",
  "research_questions_identification_level", "research_questions_data_audience",
  "publications_identification_level", "publications_data_audience",
  "project_proposal_identification_level", "project_proposal_data_audience"
)

mat <- data_raw |>
  select(all_of(cols_behav)) |>
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ as.integer(replace_na(as.character(.x), "0"))))

d   <- cluster::daisy(mat, metric = "gower")
hc  <- hclust(d, method = "average")
k   <- 5
clusters <- cutree(hc, k = k)

par(mar = c(2, 2, 3, 1))
plot(hc, labels = FALSE, main = "Dendrogramme des comportements (UPGMA, distance de Gower)", xlab = "", sub = "")
rect.hclust(hc, k = k, border = c("#e41a1c", "#377eb8", "#4daf4a", "#ff7f00", "#984ea3"))
```

```{r}
#| label: comportements-individus
#| include: false
data_raw |>
  select(all_of(cols_behav)) |>
  mutate(across(ends_with("_identification_level"), ~ niveaux_conf[as.character(.x)])) |>
  mutate(across(ends_with("_data_audience"),        ~ niveaux_aud[as.character(.x)])) |>
  mutate(Groupe = factor(clusters)) |>
  arrange(Groupe) |>
  set_names(c(
    "Conf.", "Aud.", "Conf.", "Aud.",
    "Conf.", "Aud.", "Conf.", "Aud.",
    "Groupe"
  )) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = TRUE, font_size = 12) |>
  add_header_above(c(
    "Profil chercheur" = 2,
    "Questions de recherche" = 2,
    "Publications" = 2,
    "Proposition de projet" = 2,
    " " = 1
  ))
```


```{r}
#| label: profils-moyens
col_labels <- c(
  "Conf.", "Aud.", "Conf.", "Aud.",
  "Conf.", "Aud.", "Conf.", "Aud."
)

data_raw |>
  select(all_of(cols_behav)) |>
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ as.integer(replace_na(as.character(.x), NA_character_)))) |>
  mutate(Groupe = factor(clusters)) |>
  group_by(Groupe) |>
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    across(all_of(cols_behav), ~ {
      tbl <- sort(table(.x), decreasing = TRUE)
      modal_val <- as.integer(names(tbl)[1])
      if (endsWith(cur_column(), "_identification_level")) {
        niveaux_conf[as.character(modal_val)]
      } else {
        niveaux_aud[as.character(modal_val)]
      }
    }),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  setNames(c("Groupe", "N", col_labels)) |>
  kable(caption = "Profil modal par groupe") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = TRUE, font_size = 12) |>
  add_header_above(c(
    " " = 2,
    "Profil chercheur" = 2,
    "Questions de recherche" = 2,
    "Publications" = 2,
    "Proposition de projet" = 2
  ))
```

### Comportements par instrument

Les choix de confidentialité et d'audience sont-ils stables entre instruments, ou les participants adaptent-ils leurs préférences selon le type de données ?

Le test de Cochran Q exige des réponses complètes sur tous les instruments, ce qui réduit ici l'échantillon à ~15 individus et pénalise sa puissance. Le test de Stuart-Maxwell est préférable : il s'applique par paires d'instruments et tolère les données manquantes. Il teste l'hypothèse de symétrie marginale — autrement dit, si la distribution des réponses est la même pour les deux instruments de la paire.

```{r}
#| label: stuart-maxwell
cols_conf <- c(
  "researcher_profile_identification_level",
  "research_questions_identification_level",
  "publications_identification_level",
  "project_proposal_identification_level"
)
cols_aud <- c(
  "researcher_profile_data_audience",
  "research_questions_data_audience",
  "publications_data_audience",
  "project_proposal_data_audience"
)

stuart_maxwell <- function(x, y) {
  d <- data.frame(x = as.integer(x), y = as.integer(y)) |> na.omit()
  cats <- sort(unique(c(d$x, d$y)))
  k <- length(cats)
  if (k < 2) return(tibble(stat = NA_real_, df = NA_integer_, `p-value` = NA_real_, n = nrow(d)))
  T_mat <- table(factor(d$x, cats), factor(d$y, cats))
  d_vec <- rowSums(T_mat) - colSums(T_mat)
  S <- matrix(0, k, k)
  for (i in 1:k) for (j in 1:k) {
    S[i, j] <- if (i == j) rowSums(T_mat)[i] + colSums(T_mat)[i] - 2 * T_mat[i, i]
               else        -(T_mat[i, j] + T_mat[j, i])
  }
  idx <- 1:(k - 1)
  chi2 <- tryCatch(as.numeric(t(d_vec[idx]) %*% solve(S[idx, idx]) %*% d_vec[idx]), error = function(e) NA_real_)
  p <- if (!is.na(chi2)) pchisq(chi2, df = k - 1, lower.tail = FALSE) else NA_real_
  tibble(stat = round(chi2, 3), df = k - 1L, `p-value` = round(p, 4), n = nrow(d))
}

insts <- c("Profil", "Questions", "Publications", "Projet")
pairs <- combn(4, 2)

res_conf <- map_dfr(1:ncol(pairs), function(i) {
  a <- pairs[1, i]; b <- pairs[2, i]
  stuart_maxwell(data_raw[[cols_conf[a]]], data_raw[[cols_conf[b]]]) |>
    mutate(Paire = paste(insts[a], "vs", insts[b]), .before = 1)
})

res_aud <- map_dfr(1:ncol(pairs), function(i) {
  a <- pairs[1, i]; b <- pairs[2, i]
  stuart_maxwell(data_raw[[cols_aud[a]]], data_raw[[cols_aud[b]]]) |>
    mutate(Paire = paste(insts[a], "vs", insts[b]), .before = 1)
})

bind_rows(
  res_conf |> mutate(Variable = "Confidentialité", .before = 1),
  res_aud  |> mutate(Variable = "Audience",        .before = 1)
) |>
  kable(caption = "Test de Stuart-Maxwell par paire d'instruments") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

### Transitions entre instruments

```{r}
#| label: transitions-conf
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
instruments_labels <- c("Profil", "Questions", "Publications", "Projet")

bump_plot <- function(cols, niveaux_map, titre, palette) {
  data_raw |>
    select(all_of(cols)) |>
    mutate(id = row_number()) |>
    pivot_longer(-id, names_to = "instrument", values_to = "code") |>
    mutate(
      instrument = factor(instrument, levels = cols, labels = instruments_labels),
      niveau = niveaux_map[as.character(code)]
    ) |>
    drop_na(niveau) |>
    count(instrument, niveau) |>
    mutate(niveau = factor(niveau, levels = rev(names(sort(table(niveau)))))) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = instrument, y = n, color = niveau, group = niveau)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.8, size = 3.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = palette) +
    labs(title = titre, x = NULL, y = "Participants", color = NULL) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

bump_plot(cols_conf, niveaux_conf, "Confidentialité par instrument", "Set2")
```

```{r}
#| label: transitions-aud
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
bump_plot(cols_aud, niveaux_aud, "Audience par instrument", "Set1")
```

### Conclusion

Les chercheurs adopte à plus de 80% pour une confidentialité minimale de leurs données. Ils veulent être identifiés, sauf pour la proposition de projet. Une majorité d’entre eux préfèrent toutefois que leurs données soient présentées qu’à un auditoire de chercheurs, excepté pour les publications.

Nous pourrions envisager une disposition commune pour toutes les données recueillies : identification nominative réservé à un auditoire de chercheurs.
